key_highlights: Iran war tensions and Hormuz crisis drive oil prices higher, fueling global inflation fears and bond selloffs.
Trump-Xi summit focuses on trade, rare earths, and a potential 500-jet deal for Boeing, with Nvidia chip sales to China cleared.
Inflation angst spikes yields, stocks hit, and emerging markets fall sharply as oil jumps.
Cerebras AI chip IPO soars 90%, highlighting strong AI infrastructure demand.
Ecobank seeks yuan link to reduce dollar dominance in Africa trade, signaling de-dollarization trends.
Gautam Adani nears SEC settlement, reviving dollar bond plans for Adani Group.
Global bond selloff worsens, gilts slump on political risk, and BOK flags housing risks.
Clean energy seen as structurally immune to fossil fuel shocks, with key investments accelerating.

sectors.trend: Oil prices head for weekly gain as Iran war negotiations stall and Hormuz crisis persists, driving inflation fears. Metals from copper to gold slump on inflation angst and dollar strength.
sectors.implications: Higher energy costs pressure margins across sectors, boost clean energy investments, and increase geopolitical risk premiums. Gold's decline suggests liquidity crunch or dollar strength outweighing safe-haven demand.

sectors.trend: AI infrastructure remains a bright spot: Cerebras IPO soars 90%, Nvidia CEO's foundation buys $108M in AI computing from CoreWeave, and Lightspeed appoints new CTO for AI strategy. US clears H200 chip sales to 10 China firms.
sectors.implications: AI capex cycle continues, but geopolitical tensions around chip exports and rare earths create bifurcation. AI infrastructure stocks remain attractive, but regulatory risks persist.

sectors.trend: Global bond selloff worsens as oil-driven inflation fears push yields higher. Gilts slump on political risk (Burnham challenge), dollar rallies to best week since March. Yen spikes trigger intervention speculation.
sectors.implications: Rising yields challenge equity valuations, especially growth stocks. Dollar strength pressures EM currencies and commodities. Bond investors face relentless pressure from oil prices.

sectors.trend: EM stocks fall most since March as oil jumps, Korea's rally stumbles, India braces for fuel hikes, and Ghana's rally fuels bank IPO prospects. China markets little changed after Trump-Xi meet.
sectors.implications: Oil-importing EMs (India, Korea) face stagflation risks. De-dollarization efforts (Ecobank yuan link) and rare earth dependencies create structural shifts. Selective opportunities in commodity exporters.

sectors.trend: Grocery prices jump most in nearly four years, Coles misled shoppers in Australia, and homebuying remains challenging despite high mortgage rates. Car subscription services fail to take off.
sectors.implications: Inflation is hitting consumers hard, especially at the grocery store. Retailers face margin pressure and regulatory scrutiny. Housing market remains tight with high rates.

sectors.trend: Boeing may win back China with 500-jet deal after Trump-Xi summit. Pentagon has less than 8 months to break China's rare earth grip. US soldiers learn drone identification.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical tensions drive defense spending and rare earth security. Boeing's China exposure is a key catalyst. Rare earth supply chain reshoring is critical for defense tech.

key_themes: Iran War & Hormuz Crisis: Oil spike, inflation, clean energy acceleration
Trump-Xi Summit: Trade, rare earths, Boeing jets, Nvidia chip sales
Global Inflation Angst: Bond selloff, yield spike, dollar strength
AI Infrastructure Boom: Cerebras IPO, Nvidia, CoreWeave, chip demand
De-dollarization: Ecobank yuan link, Adani dollar bonds, EM currency shifts
Central Bank Hawkishness: BOK flags housing risks, Fed on-hold path, RBI lifts bond targets
Rare Earth & Supply Chain Security: Pentagon urgency, US semiconductor reshoring
Consumer Strain: Grocery inflation, housing affordability, fuel hikes in India

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to clean energy and domestic energy producers; hedge with oil futures or energy sector ETFs; reduce exposure to oil-importing EM equities.
Reduce duration in bond portfolios; favor floating-rate notes and TIPS; consider dividend stocks with pricing power; avoid high-growth tech with long-duration cash flows.
Diversify supply chains; invest in rare earth recycling and domestic mining; monitor semiconductor export controls; consider defense and aerospace ETFs.
Reduce exposure to oil-importing EMs; favor commodity exporters; hedge currency risk; consider local currency bonds with central bank support (e.g., RBI).
Diversify sovereign bond holdings; avoid concentrated exposure to gilts; monitor Hungary's euro adoption path; Venezuela debt restructuring is a long shot.

opportunities.recommendation: Increase allocation to AI infrastructure stocks (Nvidia, Cerebras, CoreWeave, Lightspeed)
opportunities.rationale: Strong AI capex cycle, blockbuster IPOs, and continued demand for computing power. US chip sales to China cleared, reducing regulatory overhang.

opportunities.recommendation: Invest in clean energy ETFs and companies structurally immune to oil shocks
opportunities.rationale: Hormuz crisis highlights fossil fuel dependence; clean energy seen as structurally immune. Policy support and economic incentives accelerating.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Boeing on China jet deal potential
opportunities.rationale: Trump-Xi summit puts 500-jet deal in focus; Boeing lost China market and stands to regain it. Strong catalyst for aerospace recovery.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider dividend ETFs with pricing power amid inflation
opportunities.rationale: Yield is no longer king; focus on companies with ability to pass through costs. Dividend ETFs are signaling caution.

opportunities.recommendation: Explore rare earth and domestic semiconductor manufacturing ETFs
opportunities.rationale: Pentagon urgency and US reshoring push create long-term demand. Howard Lutnick highlights 'bringing back' semiconductor manufacturing.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce equity exposure and take profits in June as BofA suggests
defensive_moves.rationale: BofA's Hartnett says stock market ripe for profit taking in June. Rising yields and inflation angst challenge equity valuations.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge with oil futures or energy sector ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Oil heads for weekly gain with Iran war at impasse. Energy sector benefits from higher prices and provides inflation hedge.
Global bond selloff worsens, yields spike. Cash provides optionality and protection against further rate hikes. I bonds attractive as inflation heats up.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to oil-importing EM equities (India, Korea)
defensive_moves.rationale: India braces for fuel hikes, Korea's rally stumbles. Oil price spike pressures current accounts and currencies.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Freeze credit and monitor personal finances amid inflation
defensive_moves.rationale: Grocery prices jump most in nearly four years, business costs spike. Consumers should lock in fixed rates and build emergency savings.

market_outlook.short_term: Markets remain under pressure from oil-driven inflation, rising bond yields, and geopolitical uncertainty. The Trump-Xi summit provides some clarity on trade and rare earths, but Iran war tensions keep risk premiums elevated. Expect continued volatility with a defensive tilt. The Dow may retake 50,000 but faces headwinds from profit-taking in June.
market_outlook.long_term: AI infrastructure and clean energy remain structural growth themes. De-dollarization and rare earth security will reshape global trade flows. Central banks face a delicate balancing act between inflation and growth. Emerging markets with commodity exports (Ghana) may outperform oil importers. The Fed's on-hold path could shift if inflation persists.
key_catalysts: Iran war negotiations outcome and Hormuz crisis resolution
Trump-Xi summit follow-through on trade deals, Boeing jets, and chip sales
US inflation data and Fed communication (Berro suggests on-hold path)
Cerebras IPO performance and AI sector earnings
India fuel hike decisions and RBI bond target changes
Rare earth supply chain developments and Pentagon actions
UK political developments (Burnham challenge to Starmer)
watch_list: Oil prices (WTI, Brent) and Iran war headlines
Nvidia and AI chip stock performance
EM currency and bond markets (India, Korea, Ghana)
Gold and copper prices (inflation vs. growth signals)
Hungary euro adoption path and bond market impact
Consumer inflation expectations and grocery prices
Rare earth and semiconductor ETF flows
